assertions.text: The vehicle was traveling at approximately 100 km/h before the event.
The vehicle was in the left lane prior to the event.
The car directly in front of the vehicle suddenly decelerated.
The driver applied the brakes for several seconds before the collision.
The vehicle struck the car in front with its front end.
There was a single impact (the vehicle hit the car ahead once).
The front car slowed, then the driver braked, then the collision occurred.
The collision involved the vehicle's front end making contact (implying a front-end collision); no description of severe damage was provided.

verdicts.evidence: The EDR pre-crash speed samples show the vehicle speed was between [253.0 km/h](T2) at t=-5.0s and [203.0 km/h](T2) at t=-0.5s; the speed decreased by [50.0 km/h](T2) prior to impact, so a reported ~[100 km/h](T1) is lower than recorded but the telemetry confirms the vehicle was traveling well above [100 km/h](T1) before the event.

Lane position is not present in the available telemetry channels and cannot be determined from EDR speed/brake/delta-V data; therefore the claim that the vehicle was in the left lane is [unverifiable](T1).

There is no telemetry from the other vehicle; the available channels do not show another vehicle's speed or its sudden deceleration, so the statement about the front car suddenly decelerating is [unverifiable](T1).

Brake channel statistics show the brake transitioned from off to on at [t=-4.5 s](T2) and the fraction of samples with brake on is [0.9](T2), indicating the driver applied the brakes for an extended pre-crash period of several seconds prior to impact.

The crash pulse shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V with a peak cumulative longitudinal change of [‑89.0 km/h](T2) at [t=0.14 s](T2) which indicates forward-to-backward velocity change consistent with a frontal impact; additionally the EDR summary lists max longitudinal delta-V of [‑88.55 km/h](T1).
The EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded: 1](T1) and the crash-pulse data shows a single dominant delta-V peak around [t=0.14 s](T2), consistent with a single impact.

Telemetry shows the vehicle speed decreased from [253.0 km/h](T2) to [203.0 km/h](T2) and brakes were applied at [t=-4.5 s](T2), and the impact occurred at [t=0 s](T1), but there is no data from the front car to confirm it slowed first; thus the asserted sequence (front car slowed → driver braked → collision) is only partially supported by V1 timing and is otherwise [unverifiable](T1).

Sensor data indicate a frontal-oriented crash pulse with final longitudinal delta-V of [‑86.0 km/h](T2) and EDR summary max longitudinal delta-V of [‑88.55 km/h](T1); the narrative's statement that contact was at the front end and no severe-damage description was provided is consistent with the telemetry showing a high-speed frontal impact but no qualitative damage descriptions are available in the data (unverifiable).

result.rationale: Vehicle telemetry clearly records a high-speed, single, primarily longitudinal crash pulse and sustained pre-crash braking, supporting front-impact and braking assertions; lane position and the other vehicle's behavior are not observable from V1 sensors, making sequence and other-vehicle claims unverifiable.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
